perturbation.original_text: 5. Compensation; Expenses (a) As described in the Fund's Prospectus, the Fund may impose a sales charge "load" in connection with the purchase of shares of the Fund, a portion of which will be paid to S2K pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Prospectus. (b) In consideration of the marketing, sales and other related activities provided by S2K, the Distributor may compensate S2K for such services on each Fund's behalf and at the direction of each such Fund. The amount of compensation payable by the Distributor to S2K hereunder shall be determined on a class by class basis. At the direction of each Fund, ALPS or its designated agent will facilitate the payment of the applicable dealer reallowance fee to S2K in the amounts set forth in Exhibit B hereto. S2K shall perform such distribution-related activities for which such payments are appropriate under all applicable rules and regulations and shall make such occasional certification as required by the Distributor to such effect. (c) No compensation with respect to a Fund shall be due and owing hereunder until the Distributor actually receives payments from such Fund, to the extent applicable. (d) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, in no event shall S2K be entitled to receive fees or compensation that would cause a Fund's sales charges to exceed the maximum amount allowed under FINRA rules or applicable law. (e) S2K shall reimburse Distributor for all reasonable out-of-pocket expenses, including but not limited to: FINRA advertising/filing fees (including additional fees for expedited reviews as set forth in Section 2(b) herein).
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Compensation; Expenses (a) As described in the Fund's Prospectus, the Fund may impose a sales charge "load" in connection with the purchase of shares of the Fund, a portion of which will be paid to S2K pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Prospectus. (b) In consideration of the marketing, sales and other related activities provided by S2K, the Distributor is not obligated to compensate S2K for such services on each Fund's behalf or at the direction of each such Fund, the distributor at its sole discretion is free to deny compensation. The amount of compensation payable by the Distributor to S2K hereunder shall be determined at the distributor's discretion, regardless of class. At the direction of each Fund, ALPS or its designated agent will facilitate the payment of the applicable dealer reallowance fee to S2K in the amounts set forth in Exhibit B hereto. S2K shall perform such distribution-related activities for which such payments are appropriate under all applicable rules and regulations and shall make such occasional certification as required by the Distributor to such effect. (c) No compensation with respect to a Fund shall be due and owing hereunder until the Distributor actually receives payments from such Fund, to the extent applicable. (d) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, in no event shall S2K be entitled to receive fees or compensation that would cause a Fund's sales charges to exceed the maximum amount allowed under FINRA rules or applicable law. (e) S2K shall reimburse Distributor for all reasonable out-of-pocket expenses, including but not limited to: FINRA advertising/filing fees (including additional fees for expedited reviews as set forth in Section 2(b) herein).
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 5(b) suggests that the Distributor *may* compensate S2K, but then clarifies that the compensation *shall be determined* on a class-by-class basis, implying a structured compensation model exists. The changed text gives the Distributor the explicit option not to compensate S2K and states it is at the sole discretion of the distributor, negating the previous indication of an established compensation structure. This change contradicts Exhibit B and also Paragraph 5 (a) by introducing the possibility of S2K not being compensated at all, conflicting with the dealer reallowance fee mentioned in exhibit B set forth in each such Fund's then-current Prospectus
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit B Fee Schedule At the direction of each Fund set forth in Exhibit A to this Agreement, ALPS or its designated agent will facilitate the payment of the applicable dealer reallowance fee to S2K (as a percentage of the offering price) in the amounts set forth in each such Fund's then-current Prospectus. Note: The following applies to all Funds set forth in Exhibit A to this Agreement with the exception of the NorthStar/Townsend Institutional Real Estate Fund Inc.: In no event will a Fund's aggregate selling commissions, dealer manager fees and distribution and servicing fees, if applicable, exceed 8.0% of the aggregate gross proceeds raised in the Fund's offering. Therefore, the Distributor's facilitation of the dealer reallowance payments set forth in each Fund's then-current Prospectus shall cease with respect to the applicable Fund as of the date such 8.0% threshold has been reached with respect to such Fund. Exhibit B
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5(b) introduces a contradiction with Exhibit B. The original text implies a structured compensation model for S2K, while the changed text gives the Distributor the discretion to not compensate S2K at all. This directly contradicts Exhibit B, which states that "ALPS or its designated agent will facilitate the payment of the applicable dealer reallowance fee to S2K...in the amounts set forth in each such Fund's then-current Prospectus." If the distributor has the sole discretion to deny compensation as stated in the changed text of section 5(b), this makes Exhibit B unenforceable because the dealer reallowance fee may not be paid if the distributor elects to not compensate S2K.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Duration and Termination. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall end on the 60th day following a written notice from one party to the other of its decision to terminate this Agreement at the end of such 60-day period or upon termination of the applicable Distribution Agreement with respect to a Fund. Termination of this Agreement as to a Fund shall not terminate this Agreement with respect to any other Fund so long as such other Fund's (or Funds', as the case may be) Distribution Agreement is effective. If this Agreement is terminated by one party, it shall terminate the entire Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Duration and Termination. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall end on the 60th day following a written notice from one party to the other of its decision to terminate this Agreement at the end of such 60-day period or upon termination of the applicable Distribution Agreement with respect to a Fund. Termination of this Agreement as to a Fund shall not terminate this Agreement with respect to any other Fund so long as such other Fund's (or Funds', as the case may be) Distribution Agreement is effective. Even if this Agreement is terminated by one party, it does not automatically terminate the entire agreement, and each individual aspect of the agreement can remain in effect at the discretion of the distributor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 4 clearly states "If this Agreement is terminated by one party, it shall terminate the entire Agreement." The added text stating "Even if this Agreement is terminated by one party, it does not automatically terminate the entire agreement, and each individual aspect of the agreement can remain in effect at the discretion of the distributor," introduces a direct contradiction. It creates uncertainty regarding the termination process and the enforceability of the original termination clause, specifically the last sentence in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Duration and Termination. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall end on the 60th day following a written notice from one party to the other of its decision to terminate this Agreement at the end of such 60-day period or upon termination of the applicable Distribution Agreement with respect to a Fund. Termination of this Agreement as to a Fund shall not terminate this Agreement with respect to any other Fund so long as such other Fund's (or Funds', as the case may be) Distribution Agreement is effective. Even if this Agreement is terminated by one party, it does not automatically terminate the entire agreement, and each individual aspect of the agreement can remain in effect at the discretion of the distributor.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that termination by one party terminates the entire agreement. The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that even if terminated by one party, the entire agreement does not automatically terminate, and individual aspects can remain in effect, creating a clear and irreconcilable inconsistency with the previous statement found within the same 'Duration and Termination' section, thus causing a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Fund Materials. S2K shall be entitled to produce materials ("Fund Materials") for use in marketing a Fund as described herein, so long as the Fund Materials are produced, reviewed, principally approved, used and filed, where necessary, in accordance with FINRA and SEC regulations and those of any jurisdiction in which a Fund is solicited through use of the Fund Materials. All expenses and costs attributable to the foregoing provision shall be borne by S2K in accordance with Section 2 and Section 5 herein. S2K shall remain liable for any representations made by it or contained in materials produced and approved by S2K for use in marketing the Funds.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Fund Materials. S2K shall be entitled to produce materials ("Fund Materials") for use in marketing a Fund as described herein, only if the distributor requests. Furthermore, S2K is only allowed to use materials produced reviewed and approved by the distributor. All expenses and costs attributable to the foregoing provision shall be borne by S2K in accordance with Section 2 and Section 5 herein. S2K shall remain liable for any representations made by it or contained in materials produced and approved by S2K for use in marketing the Funds.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 9 states that S2K shall be *entitled* to produce materials with the understanding that they follow guidelines. The changed text adds that S2K is only permitted if the distributor asks for it and, furthermore, only allows S2K to produce materials reviewed and approved by the distributor. These additional stipulations contradict S2K's initial entitlement by limiting S2K ability to act on its own to market the fund and therefore creates uncertainty on the level of S2K engagement and if the distributor does not approve S2K is not capable of performing it obligations under this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Fund Materials. S2K shall be entitled to produce materials ("Fund Materials") for use in marketing a Fund as described herein, only if the distributor requests. Furthermore, S2K is only allowed to use materials produced reviewed and approved by the distributor. All expenses and costs attributable to the foregoing provision shall be borne by S2K in accordance with Section 2 and Section 5 herein. S2K shall remain liable for any representations made by it or contained in materials produced and approved by S2K for use in marketing the Funds.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants S2K the entitlement to produce Fund Materials, subject to regulatory compliance. The changed text significantly alters this by making S2K's production dependent on the distributor's request and approval. This change directly contradicts the original entitlement by imposing additional restrictions and dependencies, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction within the document and limits S2K initial obligations.
